title: Nursery Rhyme. LVII. Tales.
content:
Little King Boggen he built a fine hall.
        Pye-crust, and pastry-crust, that was the wall;
        The windows were made of black-puddings and white,
        And slated with pancakes - you ne'er saw the like.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. LVII. Tales."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
